free_text: Level structure of 32P from 31P(d,p)32P reactions.
The energy resolution of the cyclotron and analysing
system was 15-20 keV.
The protons were analysed by a high resolution
magnetic spectrometer.
The protons were detected by nuclear emulsion
plates.
Targets of Li3PO4 evaporated on backing of gold leaves
were used. In some cases, when the proton groups from
the 31P(d,p)32P reaction were interferred by proton
groups from reactions in Li, or when a check for
possible impurities in the target was needed, targets
of P2O5 on gold leaves were employed. As impurities in
the Li3PO4 targets were found carbon and small amounts
of nitrogen and sodium.
At low angles scattered protons or protons produced by
scattered deuterons caused a considerable background in
the plates. Corrections for this background were
estimated from the number of registered track between
the peaks.
